title: The Moral Animal
subtitle: Evolutionary Psychology and Everyday Life
author: Robert Wright
themes: Natural selection shaped not just our bodies but our minds — including our moral intuitions
The gene's-eye view illuminates the hidden logic of human behavior
Evolutionary psychology explains both the noble and the selfish sides of human nature
Darwin's own life illustrates the principles of evolutionary psychology
Understanding our evolved nature is the first step toward transcending it
excerpt: We are special — just as special as we have always thought. But the source of our specialness is not the ability to see beyond our selfish genes. It is the ability to see the truth about our selfish genes, and to decide, consciously, whether to obey them. That is the real moral animal: an animal that can, in full awareness, rebel against the dictates of its own nature.
